_scoring_notes.kinetic_formula: 40% intercept rate (scaled 90-100% → 0-100pts) + 20% absolute debris penalty (100 - debris×8) + 20% day-over-day trend (100 - delta×10) + 20% 7-day avg trend (100 - delta×10)
_scoring_notes.social_formula: Base 100. Deductions: -5 per NCEMA alert in last 24h, -10 per full embassy departure/reconsider advisory, -5 per airport limited ops, -10 per airport closure

social_inputs.ukFCDOAdvisory: Advise against all but essential travel to UAE

sources: UAE MoD official X account — 2 alerts issued: 06:47 and 14:54 GST
Hindustan Times Dubai live blog March 22
ARN News Centre — afternoon alert confirmed
Khaleej Times — Marina tower debris confirmed, no injuries
uaeup2date Facebook — residential courtyard debris, 2 injuries
UK FCDO — advises against all but essential travel to UAE
US Embassy — reconsider travel to UAE advisory
noticias.ae — DXB operating in controlled emergency mode with safety windows

Trump 48h ultimatum to Iran on Hormuz Strait
Iran counter-threat to hit all US energy/IT/desalination infra in region

dailyBriefing: March 22 saw a significant escalation with UAE air defences intercepting 4 ballistic missiles and 25 drones in two separate waves (06:47 and 14:54 GST). Debris struck a tower facade in Dubai Marina (no injuries), two residential courtyards in Dubai (2 injuries treated), and caused incidents near Jebel Ali Port. Dubai International Airport operated in controlled emergency mode with safety windows only. Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran to fully open the Strait of Hormuz or face US strikes on Iranian power plants; Iran responded by threatening all US energy, IT, and desalination infrastructure in the region. The UK FCDO upgraded to 'advise against all but essential travel to UAE'; the US Embassy issued a 'reconsider travel' advisory. WHO confirmed 8 killed and 158 injured in UAE since conflict began. UAE government stance remains 'normal life between alerts' with no evacuation advisory issued.

schoolClosures.label: Distance Learning Only
schoolClosures.guidance: All schools remain closed for in-person attendance through March 31. KHDA-regulated Dubai schools plan to reopen April 6. Online classes continue as normal.

supermarketStock.label: Adequate — Air Freight Supplement
supermarketStock.guidance: Major chains (Carrefour, Lulu, Spinneys) remain stocked. Air freight supplements continue to offset Hormuz shipping disruptions. Monitor for price increases on imported goods.

fuelAvailability.label: Operational — Monitor Hormuz Impact
fuelAvailability.guidance: ENOC and ADNOC stations operational across Dubai. No shortages reported. Hormuz restrictions continue to create upstream supply pressure; monitor for price adjustments.
